summary: The case presents disordered daytime eating with missed meals leading to nocturnal hunger, impulsive late-night food choices, and perceived faulty hunger cues, absent dieting. Modifiable predictors span: (Bio) metabolic/nutrition-focused adjustments (meal structure, satiety optimization, sleep-related appetite regulation); (Psycho) interoceptive calibration, cognitive-behavioral self-regulation, and habit formation; and (Social) structured support and accountability. Targeting these levers in combination can improve daytime meal initiation, stabilize hunger signals, reduce unhealthy nocturnal intake, and enhance overall self-regulation without dieting or risky interventions. Each solution links a predictor to measurable outcomes and plausible timeframes, with safety considerations noted where relevant.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured daytime eating schedule with automated reminders and social accountability (buddy check-ins), plus meal templates to support consistent daytime intake.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: External cues (reminders) and social accountability reduce forgetfulness and promote routine, creating regular caloric intake during the day and preventing long fasting periods that trigger nocturnal hunger.
solutions.measurement: Meal logging rate (days with ≥1 meal), time of first meal each day, percentage of days with missed daytime meals.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns. Monitor for excessive rigidity; adjust reminders to avoid guilt if meals slip.

solutions.solution_candidate: Evening-balanced meals and a planned pre-sleep snack with emphasis on protein/fiber, plus mindful eating practices during night hunger.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved daytime caloric sufficiency and satiety signaling should dampen late-night hunger surges and reduce compensatory nocturnal overeating.
solutions.measurement: Evening hunger intensity (0-10 scale), number of nocturnal eating episodes, type/quality of night-time foods.
solutions.safety_notes: No known safety concerns; monitor for intolerance to late meals.

solutions.solution_candidate: Pre-portioning snacks and implementing a pause-and-plan cognitive-behavioral module for hunger-driven choices (healthy snack templates, rule-based decisions).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Pre-commitment and short cognitive delays reduce impulsive, energy-dense choices during hunger episodes.
solutions.measurement: Proportion of healthy vs. energy-dense snacks during night episodes; snack approval rate before consumption.

solutions.solution_candidate: Interoceptive awareness training with hunger-satiety scaling and daily hunger cue self-monitoring (mindful eating cues).
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improves calibration of internal cues and alignment between perceived hunger and actual needs, reducing misinterpretation of signals.
solutions.measurement: Hunger cue accuracy rating (cue-rating vs. actual intake), daily hunger-satiety scale entries.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; monitor for distress during interoceptive exercises.

solutions.solution_candidate: Non-dieting nutrition education and meal-structure support (normal eating framework) with templates and goal-aligned planning to reinforce regular intake without dieting.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reframing to a non-dieting approach reduces conflicting motivations and supports stable eating without restrictive dieting that could worsen irregular intake.
solutions.measurement: Adherence to non-dieting meal plan (yes/no per day), proportion of days with adherence to meal-templates.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; ensure messaging remains flexible and non-coercive.

solutions.solution_candidate: Habit formation and environmental design with external cues (daily planning app + routine review with a buddy) to reduce daytime meal omissions.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Structured routines and social accountability enhance consistency in daytime eating by offloading cognitive load and leveraging social supports.
solutions.measurement: Days with planned meals vs. actual intake; time of first meal; app engagement metrics.

solutions.solution_candidate: Sleep circadian optimization with consistent wake/sleep times, light exposure management, and bedtime routines to stabilize appetite signals.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Regular sleep improves metabolic regulation and hunger cue stabilization, reducing nocturnal hunger and misinterpreted signals.
solutions.measurement: Sleep regularity (consistent wake time ±30 min), bedtime consistency, nocturnal hunger episodes.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; monitor for sleep disruption; adjust as needed.
